Ana was born to Cuban Immigrants, is a devout Catholic, and a Republican. She’s kind, opinionated, slightly judgmental but overall, very supportive of the people she loves and cares about. She’s also known to be a hard drinker. Ana usually needs her friends persuasion in order for her to try something new. She hasn’t had the best track record when it comes to love, considering she has dated a cousin before, who cheated on her with her Aunt and she caught feelings forAaron Jacksonbut it wasn’t mutual. characters.description: Andre JohnsonLive-ActionAnimatedAppears inBlack-ishGrown-ishEpisode debutPilotPortrayed byAnthony AndersonIssac Ryan Brown(young)GenderMaleBirthdateJune 14, 1975(1975-06-14)(age 50)NicknamesDreBig DreMr. Senior Vee-peezyDr. DreMr. JOccupationAdvertising executive atStevens and LidoStatusAliveBornCompton, CaliforniaFamilyMabel Johnson(paternal grandmother)Clarence Johnson(paternal grandfather; deceased)Earl Johnson(father)Ruby Johnson(mother)Almaviligerais Johnson(aunt)Rhonda Johnson(sister)Sharon Duckworth(sister-in-law)Rainbow Johnson(wife)Zoey Johnson(Oldest daughter)Andre Johnson, Jr.(Oldest son)Jack Johnson(Older son)Diane Johnson(Youngest daughter)Devante Johnson(Youngest son)Alicia Johnson(mother-in-law)Paul Johnson(father-in-law)Johan Johnson(brother-in-law)Santamonica Johnson(sister-in-law)June Bug(cousin)Ramona(cousin)Kyra(cousin)Ronald(uncle)FriendsKris LevineMr. StevensJoshCharlie TelphyCurtis MillerGigi FranklinDaphne Lido(sometimes)Andre  "Dre" Johnsonis the main protagonist of the American sitcom television series,Black-ish. He is portrayed byAnthony Anderson. Dre considers himself the family patriarch and when he looks at his life - he sees a beautiful wife and five kids living happily in their colonial home in the 'burbs. But when he looks a little closer -- have the trappings of success brought too much assimilation for this black family? The world he sees has a much different lens than the one he would like his family to look through. Dre's politics are left leaning and he is a staunchdemocrat. In spite of this he has manysocially conservativepolitical views. Perhaps the most pervasive one of these traits is his view of himself as "the man of the house". He also had a disturbingly sexist reaction when he found out his oldest daughter was sexually active, in comparison to when he found out Junior was as well. Dre considers himself the family patriarch and when he looks at his life - he sees a beautiful wife and five kids living happily in their colonial home in the 'burbs. But when he looks a little closer -- have the trappings of success brought too much assimilation for this black family? The world he sees has a much different lens than the one he would like his family to look through.

characters.name: Arjun Shah
characters.description: Arjun ShahAppears inGrown-ishPortrayed bySean T. KrishnanGenderMaleBirthdate{{#if:  |  |StatusDeceasedFamilyRiya Shah(Wife)Vivek Shah(Son) Arjun Shahwas a character in the original Freeform seriesGrown-ish, which is a spin-off of the ABC series,Black-ish. Arjun Shah was the husband ofRiya Shah, with whom he has a son,Vivek. He was a cab driver who worked really hard to get his family into America, so his son could achieve a good education. Arjun fled a third world country, developed a high level understanding of English, put his son through college and worshipped his wife. All that aside, Vivek has shown disdain towards his father’s career choice as a cab driver. He though his father was a smart man but had no ambition and he resented him for it. After Vivek confesses to his parents that he was a drug dealer and got expelled, Arjun disowns him. Arjun Shah was the husband ofRiya Shah, with whom he has a son,Vivek. characters.description: DaveAppears inGrown-ishGenderMaleBirthdate{{#if:  |  |OccupationCollege StudentRomancesNomi Segal(Ex-Girlfriend)Daveis a recurring character in the Freeform original seriesGrown-ish. He was a former love interest forNomi Segal. Dave is portrayed by Barrett Carnahan. Dave is a super nice guy, who met Nomi at Titanium. He offered to buy her a drink, not realizing she was already on a date with the girl next to her. In an attempt to apologize, he offers to buy them both drinks but Nomi's date is rude and tells him to just leave and he does. When things go sour between Nomi and the girl, Nomi takes him up on his offer. Dave and Nomi begin dating and Nomi loves how sweet he is. He is shown to be very caring when it's revealed he made Nomi a hangover carepackage that included tylenol, pedialyte and a big cheeseburger. Things take a turn when Nomi finds out Dave is also bisexual. She continues to date him for weeks but eventually dumps him because she viewed him differently after finding out he's been with guys. Dave lets her know that her concern about him not being able to be satisfied with just her is not about him being bisexual, it's about her being insecure. She is the sister ofAliciaand the aunt ofRainbow. Denise is played byChristina Anthony.  Denise is outspoken, social, and embraces the "black stereotype". Throughout much of her life, she stepped on others for her own gain, but after being reminded of how valuable and short life is, she decides to right all of her wrongs and be a kinder person. While she rejects things that don't follow society's rules, she also encourages others like Rainbow and Alicia to break the rules and be their authentic selves. She is very blunt and sticks to her beliefs even if they don't make sense, such as believing every man named Gerard wears a headpiece. She is a girl Nomi once hooked up with. Emily was on a date withNomi SegalwhenDaveapproached them and offered Nomi a drink. Nomi politely tells him that she's on a date with Emily. When Dave tries to apolozie by buying them both drinks, Emily mistakes this for him trying to have a threesome. Even when she finds out he wasn't, she's very rude to him and tells him to leave. When Nomi tries to tell her that she was being a little too much, she expresses how much she hates when straight guys try to live out their lesbian fanatasies and hates the objectification of their people. Nomi states that she's bisexual and Emily is upset over it and tells Nomi to call her when she's done with her phase before leaving. characters.name: Jack Johnson
characters.description: Johan Alexander JohnsonBirthdateOctober 21, 2006(2006-10-21)(age 19)NameJack Alexander JohnsonFamilyAndre Johnson(father)Rainbow Johnson(mother)Zoey Johnson(Oldest sister)Andre Johnson, Jr.(Older brother)Diane Johnson(Older twin sister)Devonte Johnson(younger brother)Earl Johnson(paternal grandfather)Ruby Johnson(paternal grandmother)Rhonda Johnson(paternal aunt)Sharon Johnson (paternal aunt by marriage via Rhonda)April Johnson (maternal grandmother)Paul Johnson(maternal grandfather)Johan Johnson(maternal uncle)Santamonica Johnson(maternal aunt)Almaviligerais Johnson (paternal great-aunt)Cha-Cha Johnson (paternal cousin)Dante Johnson (paternal cousin)Carl Johnson (paternal cousin)Diamond Johnson (paternal cousin)Riley Johnson (maternal cousin)Hunter Johnson (maternal cousin)Mabel Johnson (paternal great-grandmother)Kyra (paternal cousin)June Bug (paternal cousin)Ramona (cousin)Harrison Jackson (maternal great-grandfather)Denise Johnson (maternal great-aunt)Eleanor Jackson (maternal great-grandmother)Portrayed byMiles Brown Johan “Jack” Alexander Johnsonis one of the main characters of the ABC sitcom,Black-ish. Jack is portrayed byMiles Brown. Dianeand Jack are the Johnson family's twins, 8 years old in the first episode and now currently 14. Young Jack is more of the sensitive type and seems to idolize his dad. But he said that the latter is not his role model. He is quick to give his father a compliment, even if it does come out a little awkward. Jack is correctly labeled as “innocent” in the series. He is sweet, kind, naive, and soft. However, he is easily manipulated byZoeyandJunioroccasionally and byDianefrequently. Jack serves as the comic relief in the family. Sometimes Jack acts silly, if not outright stupid. He comes up with ideas that are nearly impossible to carry out and lacks the typical intelligence for a boy his age. He is not very smart, in fact usually gets all D’s on tests and report cards. It takes him a lot of time to understand simple things, and calls his mistakes cute. However throughout the series it is referenced that he is capable of high levels of intelligence, such as when he got an A so he could get a dog or when he tried being more serious and his teacher said he had potential. Quite rarely Jack works up the nerve to stand up for himself before the people who put him down, namelyJuniorandDiane(but rarely the latter). But he prides himself on his dancing and basketball skills. His nickname, “Party Time”, a play on his great ability at dancing, gives him some self-confidence.
